schema_version: v0 — component-charter shape; migrates into the intention envelope when MAP.md Q6 lands
what: THE SECOND SUBSTRATE — the one common messaging substrate for everything, and the SOLE path for inter-device communication. Devices never hold references to each other or call each other directly; they `post` (the only way to send) and `read` (the record — inspecting a device is reading its feed). Each device has four channels: `announce` + `personal` (RECORDS of truth) and `info` + `debug` (DIAGNOSTIC). Every envelope carries its why (CP3) and its causality (`sender`, `reply_to`), so the bus is a replayable causal record. Durable transit rides `db_domain` (owner `bus`, Law 6) — the bus opens no connection of its own. `BusDevice` subclasses `BaseDevice` (CP1-CP6, Form v0 #2).
why: Because communication has EXACTLY ONE DOOR, 'inspectable + logged + common' are not features bolted onto each surface — they are automatic, by construction (Law 4). This is the exact symmetry of db_domain: db_domain is the sole path to durable STATE (owner-gated, logged); the bus is the sole path to inter-device COMMUNICATION (owner-gated, logged). Every later surface — a web feed, an MCP inspector, a debug pane — is a READ-PROJECTION of this one substrate, so observability is never a separate build and nothing is re-derived elsewhere (Law 1). A woken device rebuilds its context by reading its own feed history (horizon-of-awareness, made concrete).
invoke: Construct: `bus = BusDevice()` (or `BusDevice(table=...)` for an ephemeral/proof table). Send: `bus.post(sender=..., to=..., channel='personal', why=..., body={...}, reply_to=None)` — the sole path. Inspect: `bus.read(to='dev', channel='announce')` returns the full-truth feed; `bus.digest(to='dev', channel='debug')` collapses a DIAGNOSTIC channel to count + tail (it refuses to collapse a record channel). Prove: `python3 cairn/bus/proofs/test_bus.py` (exit 0 = green).
node_class: device — the second universal substrate on the spine, the peer of db_domain. Where db_domain owns durable state, the bus owns inter-device communication.
entry: FRESH design, metaphor quarried from UU (the Murderbot-feeds model — announce/personal/info/debug per device — and UU's Postgres-backed message bus with envelopes). What is NEW and Cairn's: comms as a SUBSTRATE with a single door (post), symmetric with db_domain; durable transit RIDING db_domain rather than opening its own store (one-owner + logged for free); the record-vs-diagnostic distinction enforced as physics (Law 7) — a record channel refuses to collapse, only a diagnostic VIEW may; every envelope carrying why + causality so the bus is a replayable causal record, not raw traffic.
state_and_history: compiled to state.json (projected window) + history.json (append-only voyage) beside this charter — the charter-state-history split; this file stays pure intention+why, the operational voyage lives in the sidecars (Law 1/4/7)
filed_edges: (a) the WIRE PROTOCOL is a swappable adapter — an MCP adapter at the edge is a filed edge (the substrate must not be held hostage to a protocol)
(b) PER-DEVICE-OWNED channels — making each device the owner of its own inbound channel waits on a real multi-owner need
(c) RETENTION — a durable rolling-window expiry of diagnostic channels waits on real volume
(d) the HUMAN as a native participant (Akien gets channels like any device) — the channel shape is here, wiring Akien's feeds is the web-server stone
gates: proof gate — proofs/test_bus.py, runnable bare (python3) and under the tester. Durability gate: not done until proven AND committed.
falsifier: RED, by physics, on any of: (1) a posted message does not read back, or loses its why / sender / reply_to (a hollow bus that dropped or forgot a message — Law 5); (2) an unknown channel is accepted, or a whyless message is accepted (CP1 / CP3); (3) a RECORD channel collapses into a digest, OR a diagnostic `digest` alters what `read` returns (the substrate collapsing rather than only the view — Law 7); (4) a non-`bus` writer can write the transit table (the owner-gate not holding — Law 6), or the bus opens its own DB connection instead of riding db_domain; (5) `BusDevice` stops subclassing `BaseDevice` / drops CP1-CP6, or `introspect()` stops reporting intention → state → settings in order.
how_it_learns: Yes. (1) The bus IS the learning substrate for the whole fleet: because every inter-device message passes through one door as an owned, why-bearing, causal record, the trail of what the system actually said and why is greppable evidence, not memory — a device that stops posting is a signal, a channel that floods is a signal, both visible in the record rather than in silence (the UU split-between-bus-and-web-server disease, closed). (2) The record-vs-diagnostic seam is itself a learning stance: by naming which channels are records of truth (never collapse) and which are diagnostic (may collapse) BEFORE there are many surfaces, the system learns which surfaces it needs and adds them as VIEWS of the one substrate, never re-deriving traffic elsewhere. Its own horizon: the four channels and the digest shape are best-guesses-with-an-expiry — they firm up as real consumers (the system device's pokes, the web server's feeds, an MCP adapter) exercise them.
traces_to: founding intention (get Akien out of running the system — the human is a native participant on the bus, not an operator through a side door)
Law 4 (single-door comms makes inspectable+logged+common physics, not per-surface policy)
Law 6 (durable transit is an owner-gated write through db_domain)
Law 7 (record-of-truth channels never collapse; only a diagnostic VIEW may — the substrate always stores full truth)
Law 5 (intent + code + proofs share this address; and every envelope carries its why)
Law 1 (every surface is a read-projection of the one substrate — nothing re-derived elsewhere)
the converged architecture (CairnCommons/intentions-other/I-heartbeat-callbacks-and-bus.md — the two substrates)
db_domain (the peer substrate the bus is symmetric with, and rides for transit)
owner: CC owns the bus code + its proofs and gates writes to them. The bus owns its transit table (owner `bus`) through db_domain; it is the sole writer of traffic, on behalf of attributed senders.
proof: proofs/test_bus.py — a message round-trips with its why + causality and in causal order; an unknown channel and a whyless message are refused; a record channel refuses to collapse while a diagnostic channel collapses to count + tail without altering the record; transit is owner-gated through db_domain; device-hood + Form v0 #2 order. Green bare (python3) and green under the tester: `python3 cairn/bus/proofs/test_bus.py`.
provenance: Born 2026-07-18 (evening), the first NEW component of the heartbeat+callback+bus rework — built after the design conversation with Akien converged the two-substrate model (the heartbeat and the bus). Built before the ground_loop strip-down and the shim's callback-firing because both fire ONTO the bus: the substrate comes first. Next in the rework: the heartbeat (ground_loop stripped to the pulse), the shim's per-pulse callback-firing, and the system device (system_rackmount) that advertises/serves threshold callbacks by poking the bus.
